title: Remove Tote Sensor
summary: Safely remove the tote sensor from the tipper by first performing lockout/tagout using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure, then disconnecting the M8 connector and M3 socket-head screw, and finally pulling the sensor out.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the tote sensor must be removed from the tipper as documented in the source manual.
not_for: Do not use this procedure without first applying the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Do not use this procedure for tote sensor installation; this runbook only covers removal.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, disconnect the tote sensor hardware, and remove the sensor from the tipper.

safety_notes: Lock out/tag out the tipper before disconnecting or removing the tote sensor.
Do not proceed without the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2
Tools needed: 1.5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench

system_preconditions: Tipper is available for lockout/tagout before sensor removal.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2

steps.title: Lock out/tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before disconnecting and removing the tote sensor.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2 before performing any tote sensor removal work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out/tagged out in accordance with the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper has been placed in a locked out/tagged out state and is ready for safe maintenance.
steps.failure_condition: The lockout/tagout procedure is not available, not completed, or cannot be verified.

actions.label: Apply LOTO to tipper
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is unavailable.
Lockout/tagout cannot be completed or verified.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.
Do not proceed without the referenced lockout/tagout procedure.

steps.title: Disconnect the connector and retaining screw
steps.purpose: Release the tote sensor from its electrical connection and mechanical retention so it can be removed.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the M8 connector and the M3 socket-head screw.
steps.expected_result: The tote sensor is electrically disconnected and no longer secured by the M3 socket-head screw.
steps.healthy_condition: The M8 connector is disconnected and the M3 socket-head screw has been removed or released as required for sensor removal.
steps.failure_condition: The connector or screw cannot be disconnected or removed using the documented steps.

actions.label: Remove or release M3 socket-head screw

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference the example M8 electrical connector depiction and general tipper maintenance access context; this artifact is related but not confirmed as the tote sensor image.

stop_or_escalate_if: The M8 connector cannot be disconnected.
The M3 socket-head screw cannot be released or removed using the documented steps.

source_evidence.text: Disconnect the M8 connector and the M3 socket-head screw.
Escalate if the sensor cannot be disconnected or removed using the documented steps.
Nearby procedure text ... disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

steps.title: Remove the tote sensor
steps.purpose: Complete the documented removal after the sensor has been disconnected and released.
steps.expected_result: The tote sensor is removed from the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The sensor comes free from the tipper without additional undocumented disassembly.
steps.failure_condition: The sensor does not come out after the documented disconnection steps or removal requires undocumented force or additional steps.
stop_or_escalate_if: The sensor cannot be removed after disconnecting the connector and screw.
Removal appears to require undocumented steps or excessive force.

source_evidence.text: The tote sensor is disconnected and removed from the tipper.
Escalate if the sensor cannot be disconnected or removed using the documented steps.

success_criteria: The tipper was locked out/tagged out before maintenance began.
The M3 socket-head screw is disconnected or removed as required.
The tote sensor is fully removed from the tipper.
healthy_conditions: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is available and completed.
The connector and screw can be disconnected using the documented steps.
The sensor pulls out after the documented disconnection steps.
failure_conditions: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is unavailable or not completed.
The M8 connector cannot be disconnected.
The M3 socket-head screw cannot be disconnected or removed.
The sensor cannot be removed using the documented steps.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is not available.
Escalate if the sensor cannot be disconnected using the documented steps.
Escalate if the sensor cannot be removed using the documented steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Related M8 connector example and tipper maintenance access context from the same manual; not confirmed as the tote sensor.
